mnemonics.title: C.O.S.T Change Order Stages
mnemonics.content: Remember Cost of Change phases: C-Conceptualize (cheapest), O-Outline (design), S-Start (coding), T-Terminal (maintenance - most expensive)

mnemonics.title: A.G.I.L.E Values
mnemonics.content: A-G Individuals over processes, G-Working software over comprehensive docs, I-Collaboration over contracts, L-Response over planning, E-Change over rigidity

acronyms.usage: Practice of frequently merging code and automatically deploying to reduce defect detection costs
Writing tests before code to ensure early defect detection and reduce change costs
Delivering core features early to get feedback and reduce cost of building wrong features
Agile framework using iterations called sprints to manage changes efficiently

visualTips.visualization: Draw an exponential curve starting low at requirements, rising slowly through design, then shooting up during implementation and maintenance. Label the Y-axis as 'Cost' and X-axis as 'Project Timeline'
Draw two curves: Waterfall shows steep exponential rise; Agile shows a much flatter curve, demonstrating lower change costs throughout the project lifecycle
Draw an inverted cone starting wide at project start (high uncertainty in estimates) and narrowing to a point at project completion (low uncertainty)

analogies.analogy: Like building a house: changing blueprints before foundation is cheap, but changing after walls are built is extremely expensive - same applies to software
Like editing a draft essay versus writing it perfectly the first time - multiple small corrections are easier than one massive rewrite
Like maxing out credit cards for immediate purchase - convenient now but accumulates interest (future rework costs)
